title: Project Overview
description: Start here to understand what EdPath is: upload one PDF and a deterministic LangGraph workflow turns it into a guided lesson (Plan, Approve, Quiz loop, Summarize). The README documents the product flow, tech stack, and the core principle that engineering owns control flow while the LLM only fills content at generative nodes. The locked architecture reference expands this into the system-level contract, component boundaries, and data flow you will trace across the rest of this tour.

title: Backend Host & Entry
description: The backend is an Express application that hosts everything server-side. index.ts is the process entry point that creates the app and starts listening; app.ts assembles the middleware, health check, the /upload and /start routes, and mounts the CopilotKit runtime endpoint. This is the runtime skeleton every later backend piece plugs into.

title: PDF Upload & Ingestion
description: Because EdPath is grounded entirely in the uploaded PDF (no RAG or vector DB), ingestion is the source of truth for all generated content. The upload route runs multer middleware and hands the buffer to the upload service, which enforces size, magic-byte, page, and token limits. pdf-extract pulls raw text via pdfjs-dist and pdf-clean normalizes it into the `pdfText` that will ride in every prompt.

title: Lesson Bootstrap
description: Building on ingestion from Step 3, the start feature turns a validated upload into a live lesson. start.service orchestrates the flow: validate the thread id, process the PDF, build initial graph state, and seed the LangGraph thread. build-initial-state constructs the initial state object, embedding the cleaned `pdfText` as the grounding source the agent reads from.

title: Shared Zod Contracts
description: Every artifact that crosses the backend boundary is Zod-validated against contracts in packages/schemas, the single source of truth shared by both ends. The barrel re-exports each contract; lesson-plan, mcq, feedback, and summary define the shapes of the four things the agent produces. Notice that mcq.ts also exposes a PublicMCQSchema, an early signal of the assist firewall you will meet later.
languageLesson: Zod validators double as the source of TypeScript types via z.infer, so one schema definition guarantees runtime validation and compile-time types stay in lock-step across the web and backend workspaces.

title: State Types & The Firewall
description: The types package composes the schemas from Step 5 into the state objects that flow through the system. state.ts defines two shapes: the full server-side EdPathState (holds `pdfText` and full MCQs including the correct answer) and the structurally firewalled CoAgent state mirrored to the browser. phase.ts enumerates the deterministic lifecycle stages (plan, approve, quiz loop, summarize) that drive the graph.
languageLesson: The assist firewall is enforced at the type level: a distinct CoAgent state type structurally omits correctIndex/explanation/hint, so leaking answer data to the client becomes a compile error, not just a runtime check.

title: LangGraph State Annotation
description: annotation.ts is the most depended-upon file in the codebase: it defines the LangGraph State annotation that is the single source of truth for the workflow and is mirrored to CopilotKit. graph-update.ts wraps each node's return with a fresh CoAgent snapshot so the UI mirror stays in sync, and constants.ts holds the workflow bounds (like max attempts and max help turns) shared across nodes.
languageLesson: A LangGraph Annotation declares each state channel with a reducer that decides how concurrent node updates merge, making state evolution explicit and deterministic rather than last-write-wins.

title: The Teaching Graph & HITL Gates
description: graph.ts is the heart of EdPath: a StateGraph that wires every teaching node together with deterministic conditional edges and two interrupt gates. The LLM never chooses the next step; code-owned edges route the Plan, Approve, Quiz loop, and Summarize flow. The approval-gate node pauses to present the plan for human approval, and the await-input node pauses the quiz loop to wait for an answer or a help request.
languageLesson: LangGraph interrupts implement human-in-the-loop by suspending the graph at a node and persisting state via the checkpointer; execution resumes later with the user's decision, so the workflow can span multiple HTTP requests.

title: Generative Nodes
description: These are the only places the LLM does creative work, and each is bounded to filling content within the deterministic flow from Step 8. plan builds a PDF-grounded lesson plan of objectives; generate-mcq produces PDF-grounded quiz cards for the current objective; summarize assembles the end-of-lesson report. All of them draw from the system prompts collected in prompts/index.ts and stay grounded in `pdfText` rather than general model knowledge.

title: Deterministic Grading & Assist Firewall
description: Grading and the help side-channel are the two places correctness cannot be left to the LLM. The grade node calls grade-answer, which compares the selected option index to the correct index in pure code and raises a GradingError on mismatch. The assist node answers a learner's question, but assist-input first strips correctIndex, explanation, hint, and sourceQuote, structurally enforcing the firewall so the help channel can never leak the answer.

title: CopilotKit Runtime Bridge
description: This layer connects the compiled graph from Step 8 to the browser. runtime.ts constructs the CopilotKit Runtime, registers the EdPath agent, and exposes it as the Express endpoint mounted back in Step 2's app.ts. edpath-langgraph-agent.ts is a custom LangGraphAgent subclass that bridges the LangGraph dev server and handles state snapshots flowing to and from the frontend.

title: Web CoAgent Bridge & Lesson Shell
description: On the client side, the dynamic lesson route wraps LessonRunner inside the CopilotKit provider, keyed by thread id. useCoAgentLesson is the CoAgent bridge that mirrors LangGraph state into React and drives the HITL interrupt/resume flow for plan approval and answers. LessonRunner reads the current phase (from Step 6's Phase union) and decides whether to render the plan, quiz, or summary.
languageLesson: CopilotKit's useCoAgent hook subscribes a React component to the agent's shared state; the frontend receives only the firewalled CoAgent state from Step 6, so the client literally never holds the correct answer.

title: Interactive Widgets
description: These are the custom widgets the learner actually touches, driven by the phase LessonRunner selects. PlanWidget presents the generated objectives for human approval; McqWidget composes the one-card-at-a-time quiz; FeedbackBanner renders green/red functional feedback (a behavior signal, not styling); and HelpInput is the bounded 'help me' chat wired to the firewalled assist channel from Step 10.

title: Evals & Firewall Assertions
description: The capstone is the evaluation harness that guards the guarantees made throughout this tour. run-scenario drives a full lesson through the LangGraph workflow, approving plans, answering MCQs, and exercising help turns while capturing state snapshots. The assist-leakage evaluator deterministically asserts the firewall from Step 10 held, and the llm-judge scores generative quality against PDF-grounded fixtures.
